feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Gurugram Stammtisch: Invitation to SAP Community Members for Stammtisch at DLF Cyber Hub, Gurugram
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community Members,

We are delighted to invite you to the upcoming SAP Stammtisch, Gurugram. This gathering promises an engaging evening filled with networking, insightful conversations, and collaboration. 

Date: Wednesday, 16th April 2025
Time: Starting at 6:30 PM - 8:00 PM
Venue: DLF Cyber Hub, Gurugram - 122002

During the event, we will also plan and discuss the next SAP Inside Track Gurugram, an exciting community-driven initiative that brings together SAP professionals to share knowledge, experiences, and innovations.

Kindly confirm your participation by RSVP not later than Monday, 14th April 2025.

Join us for an enriching experience as we connect and share our enthusiasm for SAP!

Ravi Grover on behalf of SAP Gurgaon Community Core Team

entry.title: Accessibility News: A Practical Guide to Using Image Captions and Alt Text in SAP Community
entry.content:
I joined the SAP Community team over 15 years ago and I have always experienced the SAP Community as a place that aims to create a supportive and inclusive environment. Developers, customers, partners, and others freely collaborate and support and engage with each other. This collaboration is also possible thanks to established guidelines, which promote several key behaviors.

Below are just a few guidelines from the SAP Community rules of engagement: 

  • Share knowledge and experience   
  • Show gratitude when others share their knowledge and contribute  
  • Be respectful and professional during all community interactions   

With that said, Digital accessibility is equally important to ensure that everyone can fully participate in the SAP Community platform. 

 

Digital Accessibility and the European Accessibility Act 

Accessibility became especially significant this year with the upcoming European Accessibility Act. This directive will unify accessibility requirements across all European member states for digital services, including online platforms like SAP Community. Taking effect June 28, 2025, it emphasizes making content accessible to persons with disabilities, which is why our team is encouraging the use of the caption feature in the SAP Community platform to support alternative text for screen readers. 

Continue reading to learn about: 

  • Understanding Alternative Text (Alt Text): What it is and why it matters for accessibility 
  • Creating Effective Alt Text: Best practices and examples 
  • Using Captions to Support Alt-Text Functionality in SAP Community: How to add captions that work with screen readers 

 

Understanding Alternative Text 

Alt text is a short description of what appears in an image on a website. It describes the image’s content and its function or purpose within the context. While alt text doesn’t normally appear on screen for most viewers, it’s essential for people using screen readers—tools that convert written text to speech. Alt text is also helpful when images don’t load properly, as the description will appear instead of the image.   

 

Creating Effective Alt Text  

Best Practices for Writing Alt Text: 

  • Be specific but concise - Describe the image clearly while keeping the text brief (up to two sentences) 
  • Use keywords, but avoid keyword stuffing  
  • Don't use images as a replacement for text  
  • Avoid duplicating text that already exists in the document or website 
  • Avoid phrases like “image of” or “picture of,” but mention if it’s a logo, illustration, painting, or cartoon 

 

Why These Practices Matter: Screen Readers and Alt Text 

Screen readers are software applications that transform written text into speech, enabling people with visual disabilities to access digital content. While screen readers can automatically read text, they cannot understand images without help. This is why alt text is so important—it provides the description that screen readers need to tell users what’s in an image. When encountering an image, a screen reader will announce “image” and then read the alt text.  

 

Examples of Alt Text 

A grey kitten with black stripes playing with a colorful toy feather.

  • Good: A Kitten.  
  • Better: A Kitten playing.  
  • Best: A grey kitten with black stripes playing with a colorful toy feather.  

 

A brown horse running on green grass alongside a wooden fence. 

  • Good: A horse.   
  • Better: A horse running.  
  • Best: A brown horse running on green grass alongside a wooden fence.  

These examples demonstrate how adding specific details creates more meaningful descriptions while keeping them concise. The level of detail you choose should depend on the image’s purpose in your content. Images that deliver key information or serve a specific function may require more descriptive alt text that captures their significance while maintaining brevity.  

 

Using Captions to Support Alt-Text Functionality in SAP Community  

The SAP Community platform uses the caption feature to support alt-text functionality. This feature encourages better image descriptions and benefits all users. Adding a caption to an image ensures it is visible below the image for all viewers and read by screen readers as alt text in the HTML code. 

This is how the UI looks when you add an image, and the arrow shows where you would add the caption: 

Screenshot of blog post creation UI, showing where the alt. text will be added

Here’s how the caption appears in the post:   

Screenshot of how alt text appears in the blog post

And this is how that same caption appears in the alt text attribute in the HTML code: 

 Screenshot showing how the alternative text looks within the html view of the post

Step-by-Step: Adding Image Captions in SAP Community 

Below is a step-by-step guide on how to add captions to images in the SAP Community platform. The example shows the blog post creation interface, but it’s relevant for all content creation scenarios in SAP Community. 

  1. Accessing content creation interface 
  • Go to community.sap.com  
  • Click on a category (for example CRM and Customer experience)
  • Click on “Create a Post”:  

Screenshot showing the position of a Create a Post button in a community category page

  • Click on “Choose a board”:  

Screenshot of the blog creation UI where to click in order to choose a board

  •   Click on “Show all”:  

Screenshot of the blog post creation UI showing how to view all boards you can select from

 
  • Select the board where you would like to post your content:   

Screenshot of the blog post creation UI showing how to select the board in which you want to create your blog post

 

2. Positioning your image 

- Position your cursor where you want the image to appear 

- From the formatting toolbar at the top of the editor, select the Camera icon (labeled “Insert Photos” in the tooltip) 

- This will open the choose file dialog window 

Position your cursor where you want to place the image and click on the insert images button (Camera icon)  

3.Selecting image file 

  •  Click on “Choose Files”:  

Screenshot of blog post creation UI showing where to click in order to choose files

  • Select the image you want to use and click “Open”:   

 4.Adding caption: 

After uploading, you'll see a field for entering the caption:

  • Now Put the cursor in white space below the image 
  • Enter the text according to the best practices shared above 
  • Follow the best practices discussed earlier. 
  • Click “Done” 

Screenshot of blog post creation UI showing where you should add the alt text

  • Click “Post”:  

Screenshot of the blog post UI showing where to click in order to post the blog post

Once published, this is how your image will appear with the caption below it:  

Screenshot showing the published blog post and the position of the alternative text

Creating a Community Where Everyone Belongs 

Now that you’ve learned how to add captions to images in the SAP Community platform, I encourage you to make this a regular practice whenever you share content. By taking this simple step, you’re helping make our community more welcoming, inclusive, and accessible for everyone.  

Resources

Check out these resources if you’d like to learn more about accessibility and alt-text best practices:

  • Write helpful Alt Text to describe images (Harvard University)
  • Alt text for informative images (Siteimprove)
  • Step-by-Step guide: Adding Image Captions in SAP Community (SAP Community). It's similar to the steps shared in this blog post but please subscribe to the guide to be updated when it's enhanced. 

Please be sure to follow the Accessibility tag and visit the SAP Community topic page for Accessibility to stay updated on accessibility-relevant content.    

Before closing, I would like to thank @KimberlyMcGee for her great insights while writing this blog post.

Thank you for joining the efforts in making the SAP Community more accessible. Please feel free to raise any questions in the comments section below.

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Joined AI SAP CodeJam event, June 19th 2025, with Nora and Kevin
entry.content:
We have something special for you!

VINCI Energies reached out to us with the ask that they wanted to host a double trouble CodeJam which is all about AI. That is where @noravonthenen and I @KRiedelsheimer joined up to execute on two CodeJams at the same time:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and SAP generative AI Hub
  • Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

We can host 50 people in total, which makes it 25 each!

Not only will be there for the CodeJams which are happening June 19th, in Saint-Denis 🇫🇷 France but we will also be guests at the SAP Stammtisch event one day prior in Paris on June 18th. Details on the SAP Stammtisch will follow.

If you are interested in joining us for a full day of learning, make sure to follow one of each links to register. Please do not register for both.

See you in Paris!

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 Desafios da Reforma Tributária Brasileira
entry.content:
No contexto do evento SITCPS 2025, a palestra "Desafios da Reforma Tributária Brasileira" trouxe reflexões essenciais sobre as transformações que estão por vir no cenário fiscal do país. Disponível no YouTube, o conteúdo é uma excelente oportunidade para profissionais da área tributária, contábil e tecnológica entenderem o que muda e como se preparar.

Resumo da discussão:

A apresentação contou com dois especialistas de peso:

  • Thamires Kuwahara, com 15 anos de experiência em soluções fiscais SAP, destacou os principais objetivos da reforma, como o conceito de não cumulatividade, o split payment e os impactos na operacionalização fiscal das empresas. Thamires trouxe uma perspectiva prática e técnica, focando em como adaptar os sistemas à nova legislação.

  • Marcelo Pudenz, Diretor-Geral da Phoron Brasil, compartilhou uma visão estratégica sobre os prazos do governo e o cronograma da reforma. Além disso, ofereceu dicas importantes sobre planejamento e gestão de mudanças, essenciais para que empresas consigam se antecipar aos desafios e evitar surpresas.

A palestra reforçou a importância de não apenas compreender o texto da reforma, mas também de se antecipar com uma boa estratégia de adequação e atualização dos sistemas e processos internos.

entry.title: Awesome SAP Stammtisch Gurugram 16-April-2025 - Thank you!
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community Members,

A big thank you to everyone (Neeraj, Puneet, Aditi, Aayush, Shweta, Chandra, Parv, Ashutosh, Yash, Niladhari, Abhishek, Akshay and Jaspal) who joined us for the SAP Stammtisch Gurugram on Wednesday, 16th April 2025, at DLF Cyber Hub. Your enthusiastic participation made it a truly energizing and collaborative evening!

It was wonderful to reconnect, share ideas, and engage in meaningful conversations around our shared passion for SAP. The positive energy, open discussions, and friendly vibes made the event memorable — we deeply appreciate each one of you for being part of it.

A special shout-out to those who contributed their thoughts and suggestions during our planning conversation for the upcoming SAP Inside Track Gurugram. Your inputs around session formats, speaker suggestions, and focus areas (especially the emphasis on AI, BTP, and real-world customer use cases) were incredibly valuable. It's exciting to see the level of community spirit and commitment toward making the next SIT event even more impactful.

Let’s keep the momentum going and continue building this strong SAP ecosystem together. If you missed this one, no worries — we hope to see you at the next Stammtisch and, of course, at SAP Inside Track Gurugram 2025 (details coming soon)!

Until next time — keep learning, keep sharing.

Warm regards,
Ravi Grover
On behalf of the SAP Gurgaon Community Core Team

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 O Fim da TAXBRA/RVABRA e a Chegada do TaaS: Tudo o Que Você Precisa Saber
entry.content:
Palestrante: Fabio Dezan

Título da palestra: O fim da TAXBRA/RVABRA e a chegada do TaaS

Descrição oficial: A apresentação abordará o fim da TAXBRA/RVABRA e a introdução do Tax Engine (TaaS), com uma demonstração prática das diferenças entre a TAXBRA e o Cálculo Externo de Impostos.

Quem é Fabio Dezan?

Fabio Dezan é consultor SAP MM com especialização em S/4HANA e Tax Engine (TaaS). Com uma carreira sólida e vasta experiência prática, ele é referência quando o assunto é transformação fiscal dentro do ecossistema SAP no Brasil.

Palestra técnica, prática e inspiradora. A apresentação mistura conteúdo técnico avançado com uma narrativa pessoal inspiradora, mostrando sua trajetória profissional e como eventos da comunidade SAP moldaram sua carreira.

O que muda com a chegada do TaaS? A palestra é um verdadeiro mapa para entender como a SAP está substituindo a antiga lógica baseada em TAXBRA e RVA BR por um modelo mais eficiente, conectado e moderno: o TaaS (Tax as a Service). O cálculo de impostos sempre foi feito pela fórmula 320, e agora será feito pela nova fórmula 279, com o apoio de motores externos homologados, como Sovos, Valara e outros.

Menos complexidade, mais precisão com o TaaS, o SAP passa a se conectar diretamente a motores fiscais externos usando arquivos JSON padronizados, e o cálculo acontece fora do sistema. Isso simplifica drasticamente o processo, elimina a necessidade de múltiplas regras manuais e traz clareza e agilidade à apuração tributária.

Técnicas práticas e exemplos reais. Inclusive comparações lado a lado entre o modelo antigo e o novo, demonstrando como o novo motor de cálculo reduz o número de IVAs, simplifica a pricing e permite maior controle e rastreabilidade sobre os dados fiscais.

Durante a sessão, o público faz perguntas cruciais, como o impacto do TaaS nas exceções fiscais e como se dará a manutenção das regras. Fabio esclarece que agora os parceiros são responsáveis por manter as regras atualizadas, e que a empresa pode cadastrar exceções diretamente no motor de cálculo, sem sobrecarregar o SAP.

Se você atua com SAP MM, SD, FI ou é consultor técnico, esta palestra é essencial. Além de trazer uma visão detalhada da transição para o TaaS, ela oferece insights valiosos para quem já trabalha ou vai trabalhar com o novo modelo.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

Note that the user assistance content is now available in 39 languages. You can benefit from real-time machine translations through SAP Translation Hub. Use the language menu to select your preferred language on the SAP Help Portal.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: O que esperar no SAP Inside Track Salvador? #SITSAL2025
entry.content:
No dia 26 de abril de 2025, Salvador se tornará o epicentro do conhecimento SAP com a realização do SAP Inside Track Salvador, um dos eventos mais aguardados pela Comunidade SAP no Brasil. Parte de uma série global organizada por membros ativos da comunidade, o SAP Inside Track tem como missão compartilhar experiências reais, fortalecer o networking e explorar o que há de mais atual nas soluções SAP

Nesta edição, o destaque será total para as inovações do SAP S/4HANA, IA generativa, APIs bancárias, Tax as a Service, Clean Core e muito mais.

O que está por vir na programação?

 

O evento conta com um time de peso composto por especialistas reconhecidos, trazendo temas técnicos, funcionais e estratégicos:

Marssel Vilaça — abre o evento com a Keynote de boas-vindas, conectando tendências, propósito e a importância do compartilhamento de conhecimento no crescimento da comunidade.

Fábio Dezan — com o tema "TaaS S/4HANA – Tax as a Service substituindo a TAXBRA e RVABRA", aborda a transformação nos processos fiscais com a chegada do S/4HANA.

Aline Damasceno — destaca “O futuro da logística operacional com SAP S/4HANA”, com foco em eficiência, visibilidade e mão de obra qualificada — pilares cruciais para a evolução logística.

Manuel Robalinho — apresenta “A inovação dos processos financeiros com APIs bancárias”, essencial para quem busca automação financeira e integração com o sistema bancário.

Eliana Lanna — compartilha lições práticas do projeto Evolution, com foco na implantação do S/4HANA na PetroRecôncavo, trazendo os bastidores de um projeto real.

Luciano Casado — conecta tecnologia e propósito com o tema "Joule: a nova experiência da SAP e por que você deveria dar match com ela".

Iliada Campos — com o tema “Deve é bom, mas conhecer o SAP ajuda a trazer grandes resultados”, mostra como o conhecimento funcional transforma a jornada profissional.

Lucas Ferreira — explora personalizações Clean Core com SAP Fiori, orientando como customizar sem comprometer a integridade do sistema.

Alexandre Guerreiro — encerra com o poderoso tema “SAP Accelerate: como a Accenture vem aplicando IA Generativa em soluções SAP”, apontando o futuro das soluções empresariais inteligentes.

Por que participar?

O SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025 é o lugar certo para quem busca:

  • Atualização profissional com conteúdo de ponta

  • Networking com especialistas, líderes e novos talentos

  • Conhecimento aplicado, direto de projetos reais

  • Inspiração para alavancar sua carreira com SAP

O evento será realizado na Sala Taperapuã, Porto Bello Hotéis, Salvador/BA.

O Evento conta com capacidade esgotada - Ficou de fora? Siga este grupo da Comunidade Salvador e fique sempre por dentro das proximas edições
www.sitBrazil.com

Se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

Organização e apoio

O evento é organizado por voluntários da Comunidade SAP e conta com o apoio de patrocinadores como SAP Press, Accenture, MakeValue, Tachyonix, GFX Consultoria e Moovi Education.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025: Tudo sobre a Programação e os Palestrantes #sitREC2025
entry.content:
O que esperar do SAP Inside Track Recife 2025?

Com o tema “It’s all about the people”, o SIT Recife 2025 reunirá especialistas que atuam diariamente em projetos SAP no Brasil e no exterior. A agenda foi cuidadosamente planejada para oferecer uma imersão em temas atuais como SAP S/4HANA, FIORI, BTP, Clean Core, Inteligência Artificial e muito mais.

 

Cada palestra foi selecionada com base em seu potencial de gerar valor imediato para os participantes — trazendo estudos de caso, experiências práticas e insights de mercado.

Agenda Confirmada

  • 08:00 – 08:30 | Credenciamento e Coffee-Break
  • 08:30 – 08:40 | Abertura Oficial
  • 08:40 – 09:10 | Marssel Vilaça – Estudo de caso com os Caminhos da Conversão para o S/4HANA
  • 09:10 – 09:50 | Fabio Dezan – Mitos e Verdades sobre a Nova Localização Brasil e o Fim da TAXBRA no S/4HANA Public Edition
  • 09:50 – 10:30 | Lucas Ferreira – Como extrair o melhor do FIORI com personalizações e Clean Core
  • 10:50 – 11:30 | Gabriel Magalhães – O Fim da SE38? O Futuro do Desenvolvimento ABAP no S/4HANA
  • 11:30 – 12:10 | Luciano Casado – Pagamento com cartões integrado ao SAP S/4HANA
  • 12:10 – 12:30 | Capgemini – Apresentação Institucional
  • 12:30 – 14:00 | Intervalo para Almoço
  • 14:00 – 14:40 | Antonio Figueredo – Sistema desenvolvido a partir do BTP para relacionamento com clientes
  • 14:40 – 15:20 | Manuel Robalinho – A Inovação na Comunicação Bancária com o SAP e o uso de IA em Finanças
  • 15:40 – 16:20 | Sérgio Ferreira – Desbravando o Futuro com SAP Analytics e Business Data Cloud
  • 16:20 – 17:00 | Edson Inowe – IA no Ecossistema SAP: Inovação, Eficiência e o Futuro da Gestão Empresarial
  • 17:00 – 17:30 | Encerramento + Sorteio de brindes

Interessado em palestrar em futuras edições?

Para esta edição, nossa agenda de palestras está completa, mas se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse:

🔗Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

 

Um evento com propósito

Mais do que uma agenda técnica, o SIT Recife é um evento colaborativo — feito por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o objetivo de compartilhar conhecimento real, formar conexões profissionais e fortalecer o ecossistema local.

Organizado de forma independente e com o apoio de empresas comprometidas com a inovação, o evento mostra a força da comunidade e sua capacidade de transformar carreiras e negócios.

  • Data: 10 de Maio de 2025
  • Local: Kastel Manibu Recife Hotel - 919 Avenida Conselheiro Aguiar Boa Viagem, PE 51011-031
  • Hashtag oficial: #sitREC2025
  • Transmissão ao vivo: Youtube SAPInsideTrack

Prepare-se para um dia de muito conteúdo, conexões e inspiração.
Nos vemos no SAP Inside Track Recife 2025!

entry.title: Paraguay presente en SAP Inside Track Campinas 2025
entry.content:
¡Qué experiencia increíble vivimos este 5 de abril en el SAP Inside Track Campinas 2025!
Como parte de la comunidad SAP Paraguay, fue un privilegio estar presente en FACAMP y sentir esa energía única que solo los eventos SIT logran crear. Nuestra delegación, aunque pequeña, fue recibida con los brazos abiertos por nuestros colegas brasileños, demostrando que el espíritu de la comunidad SAP trasciende fronteras.

Una agenda que superó expectativas
Lo más destacado del SITCPS fue sin duda su potente agenda

  • Inteligencia Artificial para Consultores Funcionales
  • Simplificación de conectividad bancaria con soluciones SAP y APIs
  • Metodología RISE with SAP
  • Joule: La nueva asistente de inteligencia artificial de SAP
  • Signavio y Lean IX (Transformación de Negocios)
  • Optimización con SAP Fiori y personalizaciones Clean Core
  • Casos prácticos de SAP Business AI

Hands-on: Aprender haciendo
Sin olvidar los increíbles talleres prácticos:

  • Workflow en SAP BTP
  • Desarrollo de aplicaciones FIORI en BTP sin código
  • SAP BTP Build

El valor real: Conexiones humanas
Además de las charlas técnicas, lo que más valoro son las conversaciones informales durante los coffee breaks - ¡ahí es donde nacen las mejores ideas!

Diferencias y aprendizajes
Me sorprendió ver lo madura que está la comunidad brasileña, con eventos SIT que atraen a cientos de participantes y patrocinadores importantes. En Paraguay, estamos dando nuestros primeros pasos, pero con gran entusiasmo. La clave parece estar en la consistencia y en crear espacios donde el conocimiento fluya libremente.

Próximos pasos para nuestra comunidad
Inspirados por esta experiencia, ya estamos planificando nuestro primer SIT Paraguay para noviembre 2025. Queremos replicar lo mejor del modelo brasileño, adaptándolo a nuestra realidad.

Gracias a Roberta Fernandes y Manuel Robalinho y todo el equipo organizador por hacer posible este encuentro. Espero que pronto podamos recibirlos en Paraguay para fortalecer aún más los lazos entre nuestras comunidades SAP.


Si eres un profesional SAP en Paraguay y aún no te has unido a nuestra comunidad, ¡este es el momento! Síguenos en LinkedIn y únete a nuestro grupo de WhatsApp para estar al tanto de nuestros próximos encuentros.

¡Nos vemos en el próximo SIT, sea en Brasil o en Paraguay!
Zami

entry.title: SAP Community Paris Meetup #8 - Recap & attendance record 🚀!
entry.content:
On April 8th, we had the pleasure of gathering for a new SAP Community Paris meetup, and what a night it was, just EPIC !!
Thanks to the amazing parisian energy, we hit a record: over 110 participants joined us at Malt’s beautiful office in Paris. It was great to see so many familiar faces, and lots of new ones too!

 

VINCI Energies: 7 Years on S/4HANA: Lessons and Next Steps

The evening started with a very interesting session from the team at VINCI Energies, who shared their experience with S/4HANA.

They made the move 7 years ago, well ahead of the curve, and gave us a deep, honest look into their experience:

  • Why they decided to migrate early
  • The challenges they faced (both technical and human)
  • What they’ve learned across multiple areas: finance, logistics, development, and architecture
  • And how their transformation is still evolving today

It was an honest and practical presentation, full of real-life examples and tips for anyone preparing or going through a migration.

 

SAP: What’s new and what’s coming

Next, Alexandre and Florian from SAP France gave us a look at the latest updates in the SAP world. They covered some exciting topics like:

  • New features in finance, like DRC
  • The evolution of GROW and RISE
  • Real-world use cases for AI, BTP, and Business Data Cloud

There was a ton of interest, and lots of great questions from the audience, a proof that the community is eager to learn and ready to move forward.

Big thanks to everyone!

This event was a success thanks to so many people:

  • VINCI Energies, for letting us present our journey and give the valuable feedback
  • SAP France,for their clear and inspiring session
  • Malt, for being such wonderful hosts (and for the drinks and food too!)
  • Espresso Tutorials, for the great ebook giveaways 📚
  • And most of all: YOU, the community, for being there, sharing your energy, and making SAP Community Paris what it is today 💙

What’s next?

We’re already working on the next events, and trust us, you won’t want to miss them.
See you soon. And until then, keep learning, keep sharing, and keep the spirit of community alive! 🔥

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Porto 2025
Have you ever heard a child say: ‘I want to become an SAP Consultant?’
entry.content:
Let’s be honest: No one dreams of becoming an SAP Consultant when they’re a child.
There’s no LEGO set for it. No cartoon character. No poster on the classroom wall - no movie.

Five years ago, SAP was a complete mystery to me – or rather, a book with cryptic chapters: S/4HANA, ABAP, PP-DS, APO, IBP or BTP.

Back then, as a humanities graduate with art background, I would’ve loved to hear someone say:
“Go for it – your perspective belongs here, too.”

Today, I know: SAP is the perfect playground for those who want to shape, not just execute.
It’s full of complex systems – but also full of potential for human-centric change.
For people who don’t just think in transactions, but in transformation.

Working primarily in the German-speaking SAP ecosystem, I’ve seen both the opportunities and the blind spots. The strong structures – and the missing stories.

I’m currently writing my first book for SAP Press, planning a doctoral research project, and hosting two podcasts for the SAP community:
🎙 SAP Sommeliers – for everyone who enjoys SAP with depth and delight, and
🍵 Alice in Serverland – my teaparty for strong women in SAP.

Myself now in my office and when I was a child.

But most importantly: I’m here to help others not just get started – but to find and live their role in the SAP ecosystem in a self-determined and strength-oriented way.
Especially those who still think: “SAP? That’s not for me.”

Because here’s the truth:
In Germany, most young people – and especially women – don’t actively aim for a career in SAP. They stumble into it.
It’s rarely a conscious choice, even though the opportunities are enormous.

With a growing skills shortage and serious talent gaps, we can’t rely on coincidence anymore.
We need to turn accidental careers into intentional ones.
And that means: Every SAP consultant should speak about what they do – outside the SAP bubble.
To make this profession more tangible, more human, more alive.

Because maybe, just maybe, the next generation will say:
“I want to become an SAP Consultant – because I’ve seen what’s possible.”

💡 Let’s make SAP more approachable for young minds and career changers – and build the kind of ecosystem where dreams don’t have to start in tech to end in impact.

👉 What opened the door for you?
👉 Who helped you see your place in this ecosystem?
👉 Who should we make more visible in the SAP Community?

Let’s connect and co-create.

Warmly,
Sarah

P.S.: This was my Hello-World-Post for the SAP Community Blog - I hope you enjoyed it 🙂

entry.title: Here come a new SAP challenger ¡
entry.content:
I'm very happy to welcome @CrisGomez to the #SAP Community

It took her 4 years to join, but after a long journey, and a heroic battle with Universal ID, she's finally here 😄
Does anyone know the best SAP Community group to discuss SAP Master Data Governance ?

Welcome aboard, Cristi ¡  ¡Enjoy this amazing community !

entry.title: Through the Looking Glass: Manage Service Orders in Joule
entry.content:
Service Management and Joule have advanced their integration. After release of the first Joule case for service confirmations with OP2023 FPS3, the Joule release 04/25 now delivers first capabilities to manage service orders. We provide now a

  • Fast navigation to a service order
  • Search service orders
  • Release a single service order
  • Release a single service order item

Motivation and Scope

Joule is the central AI assistant provided by SAP. Joule enables users to execute tasks using fluent, easy-to-use language. The capabilities in Joule for managing service orders are a first basic step for a more simple, faster way to navigate, search and release single service orders and single service order items.

This is especially interesting for users like managers which are not very confident with the more detailed Manage Service Orders app in the Fiori Launchpad.

The capabilities for service orders are assigned to the role SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER (Service Manager). The ODATA API for service orders which is called by Joule handles all authorizations as of now.

Starting Screen

The starting screen provides provides a button which opens up the Search Manage Order screen in the background.

 

Fast Navigation

Joule allows users to open service orders with simple prompts like ‘Display Service Order [Service Order ID]’. This feature displays essential details such as service order ID, description, and Sold-to-Party, with an ‘Open’ button for direct access.

If the button is chosen, the service order object page opens up directly.

Search Service Orders

Searching for service orders is now streamlined. Users can employ prompts like ‘Display service orders [search criterion] [value]’ to find orders based on criteria such as Posting Timeframe, Created By, and Changed At. 

The currently supported search criteria are:

  • Posting Timeframe
  • Created By (System User)
  • Created At
  • Changed By (System User)
  • Changed At

The search results are organized by the newest posting dates, enhancing efficiency. The button opens up the service order in the background as described in the navigational case.

The number of all displayed and existing service orders, e.g. 6/18 (6 out of 18 found) is displayed.

The five delivered search criteria—Posting Timeframe, Created by, Created at, Changed by, and Changed at—can be combined. But in the prompt currently a search criterion can be used only once.

A complex prompt example like ‘Display service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created at 11.05.2022 and changed by ZHANGBOWE1 and changed at 12.09.2022’.

The search with the criterion Posting Timeframe allows to search for a specific year, month or day.

Examples are posted last year, this year’, in 2022, current month, today, yesterday, etc.

 

Release of a Service Order

A prompt like ‘Release service order [service order ID]’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order is already released.

If it is not released, Joule attempts to release this specific service order. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below is an example of a successful release.

The example below shows an error case. The first error message of the transaction is displayed as feedback.

The example below shows a release of a service order which is already released. As can be seen, Joule checks this first and reacts accordingly.

Release of a Service Order Item

A prompt like ‘Release item [item number] of service order [service order ID].’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order item. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order item is already released.

If it is not released, Joule tries to release this specific service order item. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below an example of a successful release is given.

The error case and an already released service order item are handled similar as with a service order release.

Capability Limitations

While Joule offers significant improvements, there are limitations, Currently there are the following limitations.

Direct Navigation:

Prompts like ‘Open up service order 800023831 directly’ are currently not supported. The provided open button must always be pressed. It is not possible to open up the service order object page via prompting only.

 

Search service orders:

Same criterion cannot be used multiple times. ‘Example: Search service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created by ZHANGBOWE1.’

 

Release of service orders and service order items:

Releasing multiple service orders in one prompt is currently not supported.

Releasing multiple service order items in one prompt is currently not supported

Release of service orders and service order items together in one prompt t is currently not supported.

Direct release without confirming the change. The current concept of Joule is that the user should get a summary of the change and has to confirm it before execution.

Joule is currently not able to differentiate between header and item errors. This means the release of a service order item is currently an error case as well if there is an error in the transaction on the header or another item.

 

Outlook

Future updates aim to expand search criteria. We also aim to add additional fields to the search result list. We are thinking as well about additional info sections to drill down for further information by another click. Last but not least additional status changes ands a create service order use case is looked upon.

Conclusion:

Joule is paving the way for a more intuitive and efficient service management experience. As SAP continues to refine and expand Joule's capabilities, users can look forward to even more streamlined processes. Your feedback is invaluable, so feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions.

Thank you for exploring the new features with us. We hope this makes your service management tasks a little easier and more enjoyable.

Last but not least if you liked this blog and found it helpful, I am happy to receive your kudos.

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content:
Hello SAP family! This blog is a part of the "Blog It Forward" challenge. Thanks to my friend and almost neighbor @Lluis, and his recent blog, for tagging me to participate in this great initiative for the first time.

My name is Daniel Mateo, and I’m an experienced SAP S/4HANA Project Manager based in a coastal city near to Barcelona, with a passion for transforming business financial operations  leveraging SAP’s capabilities to create real business value and guide organizations through their digital transformation journeys.

I began my career around 20 years ago at a Spanish IT consulting firm as an ABAP developer, but I quickly transitioned into functional roles within Finance and Logistics. Over time, I specialized in international SAP rollouts, with a strong focus on Finance and Controlling. For the past 12 years, I have been working as an SAP Project Manager, leading global implementations and transformations across various sectors such as pharma, manufacturing, food and beverages, mass media, and casinos, among others. Throughout the years, I’ve had the privilege of working with diverse teams in multiple countries—combining my passion for travel with my career in SAP.

In addition to managing complex implementations, I thrive on continuous learning and adapting to the evolving landscape of SAP technologies. My journey has been shaped by both professional achievements and personal experiences, such as relocating to Bulgaria during the pandemic and staying for three years. This allowed me to embrace new challenges while contributing to SAP projects remotely.

Fun Facts About Me

  • 🌍SAP and Backpacking: A Perfect Match
    My career in SAP has taken me across the globe, and I have made the most of it by exploring over 60 countries. Whether it is backpacking through Southeast Asia, road-tripping across Europe, or discovering hidden gems in South America, I love immersing myself in new cultures. Traveling has also given me a unique perspective in my work—understanding different business environments, adapting to diverse teams, and experiencing firsthand how SAP solutions impact companies worldwide.
  • 🇧🇬Bulgaria: An Unexpected Home
    When the pandemic hit, I made the spontaneous decision to relocate to Bulgaria. What started as a temporary move turned into a three-year stay. I got to experience the country’s rich history, vibrant tech scene, and stunning nature, from the Black Sea coast to the Rila Mountains. It was an incredible time for personal and professional growth, and it reinforced how flexible and borderless the SAP world can be.
  • 🏖Yoga and Beach: Recharging My Energy
    No matter the season—winter or summer—I always find time to enjoy the beach. There's something about the sound of the waves and the fresh sea breeze that resets my mind after long hours on SAP projects. The perfect combination? Practicing yoga by the sea. It’s my go-to way to recharge and stay centered, especially when juggling tight go-lives, complex configurations, and international time zones. 

 What do you most enjoy about the SAP community?

The SAP community is an incredible ecosystem of professionals who are passionate about technology and innovation. What I enjoy the most is the collaborative spirit—whether it's through groups, events, or online discussions, there's always someone ready to share knowledge and best practices.

It is inspiring to be part of a global network that is constantly evolving and sharing the trending topics to enhance the business transformation.

I regularly participate in SAP Inside Track events (mainly in Fortaleza and Barcelona), both as a speaker and an attendee. In addition, I maintain an active presence on LinkedIn, where I share insights and experiences related to SAP. I am also the co-author of a book in Spanish that details the recommended Best Practices in SAP S/4HANA rollouts, further contributing to knowledge sharing within the ecosystem.

What source do you use the most for learning SAP technologies?

I rely on a combination of official SAP resources and community-driven content. SAP Learning Hub and SAP Help Portal are excellent for structured learning. Additionally, SAP blogs, SAP Community discussions, and LinkedIn groups provide real-world insights and practical applications from experienced professionals. Keeping up with industry leaders and engaging in discussions helps me stay ahead of emerging trends in the SAP ecosystem.

What do you think are the key elements for a successful project?

A successful SAP project is built on three fundamental pillars:

  1. Clear Business Objectives: Aligning technology with business goals ensures that the solution delivers real value.
  2. Strong Governance & Stakeholder Engagement: Continuous collaboration between IT and business teams minimizes risks and ensures smooth decision-making.
  3. Effective Change Management :The success of a project is not just about technical implementation; it’s about how well users adopt the system. Training, communication, and proactive issue resolution are critical for long-term success.

What do you enjoy most in your work and why?

What I enjoy the most is driving business transformation. Seeing how SAP solutions optimize financial processes, improve efficiency, and empower companies to make better decisions is incredibly rewarding. Every project is unique, with its own challenges and opportunities, and that variety keeps the work exciting. Also, working on international projects allows me to engage with diverse teams and cultures, which is a great learning experience.

How do you survive when SAP keeps shifting our journey to new products?

SAP’s ecosystem is constantly evolving, and while it can feel overwhelming (especially with all the new acronyms, product renamings, and shifting roadmaps), the key to surviving is to position yourself as someone who doesn’t just follow the change, but helps lead it.

Here are a few survival strategies:

  1. Stay Curious and Keep Learning: New product? New cloud solution? New “RISE with” slogan? Dive into it. Take SAP Learning Hub courses, read blog posts, join SAP Community discussions. Even if you don’t implement it tomorrow, understanding what’s coming is power.

  2. Stick to Core Principles: While tools change, the fundamentals of business processes, integration, data governance, and project management stay solid. Focus on what delivers value to the business—technology is just the enabler.

  3. Build a Solid Network: Talk to other professionals, share experiences, learn from what’s worked (or not worked) in other projects. LinkedIn, SAP Community, and internal project retrospectives can be goldmines.

  4. Be Honest with Clients and Stakeholders: Don’t pretend to have all the answers. SAP sometimes doesn’t either! But if you can bring clarity to the ambiguity, people will trust you even more.

  5. Have a Sense of Humor: When a product name changes for the third time in a year, laugh. Then update your slide deck.

How do you see SAP technology in the next 5 years?

SAP technology is evolving rapidly, and I see three key trends shaping its future:

  1. Cloud-First & AI-Driven Solutions: SAP will continue pushing towards cloud-based ERP with AI-driven automation to enhance decision-making.

  2. Increased Integration & Interoperability: With the rise of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), we will see more seamless integration between SAP and non-SAP applications.

  3. Sustainability & Compliance: SAP is heavily investing in sustainability solutions, and I expect more tools helping companies track and manage their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

I hope you enjoy reading this blog as much as I enjoyed sharing my journey. Thanks for being part of the SAP community!

I would like to Blog it forward to @Marssel700 ,  a well-established SAP Champion with a strong finance background and creator of amazing courses, and to @AAncos , one of the top voices in the SAP Spanish-speaking community who truly deserves to be part of the SAP champions. I would suggest answering the question: With SAP's continuous push toward cloud-based solutions, how do you see the role of traditional SAP consultants evolving over the next 3–5 years? I'm sure they both have plenty of surprising insights and stories to share with us! 😉

entry.title: Automatisez l’intégration des taux de marché dans SAP grâce à Market Rates Management
entry.content:
Introduction 

Dans un contexte économique en constante évolution, disposer de données financières fiables et actualisées est un véritable enjeu pour les entreprises. C’est précisément là qu’intervient le service SAP Market Rates Management, une solution proposée par SAP pour automatiser l’intégration des taux de marché dans les systèmes SAP.

Durant mes projets d’implémentation SAP en tant que lead technique, j’ai souvent été confronté à l’intégration de taux de change provenant de sources externes spécifiques développées sur mesure, sans recourir au service standard SAP Market Rates. Aujourd’hui, avec l’évolution de SAP BTP et l’arrivée de services comme celui-ci, il devient plus simple et plus sécurisé d’automatiser ces flux via une approche standardisée.

Ce blog marque le début d’une série d’articles autour des services SAP disponibles sur la SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). On commence avec un focus sur l’activation et l’intégration du service Market Rates avec SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

 

Nous verrons d’abord les différentes options proposées par SAP pour la gestion des données de marché, avant de plonger dans les étapes techniques d’activation via SAP BTP, puis de configuration dans un environnement SAP S/4HANA.

Le service SAP Market Rates Management permet d’automatiser l’acquisition, la gestion et l’intégration des données de marché dans vos environnements SAP. Il centralise les taux de change, indices financiers et autres données nécessaires à la gestion de trésorerie, à la comptabilité et aux rapports financiers.

SAP propose deux approches principales pour alimenter ces données :

  1. Refinitiv (anciennement Thomson Reuters)
    Une solution clé en main fournie par SAP, qui s’appuie sur une source de données financière globale. Elle garantit une actualisation automatique et fiable des taux directement depuis le marché.
  2. Bring Your Own Rates (BYOR)
    Une option qui permet aux clients d’importer leurs propres données depuis des sources internes ou partenaires, via des intégrations personnalisées.

 

Dans ce blog, nous nous concentrerons exclusivement sur l’option Refinitiv, qui permet une intégration rapide et standardisée. L’approche BYOR fera l’objet d’un prochain article.

 

 

Activation du service dans SAP BTP

L’activation du service SAP Market Rates Management se fait via le cockpit SAP BTP. Voici les étapes à suivre pour le mettre en place dans votre sous-compte Cloud Foundry

  1. Activation des services nécessaires

Activez les deux plans de service suivants (à adapter selon votre environnement) :

  • Refinitiv  (market-rates-th): service principal pour la gestion des données de marché
  • SAP Market Rates Management  (market-rates-ui 😞 interface utilisateur (tuiles Fiori)
  1. Création de l’instance et de la clé de service
  • Créez une instance pour chaque plan via le cockpit
  • Générez une clé de service pour obtenir les informations d’authentification OAuth nécessaires (Client ID, Secret, URL, scopes...).

 

Les paramètres Client ID, Client Secret ainsi que l’URL (complétée par le suffixe /oauth/token) seront utilisés pour la génération du token d’authentification OAuth2.

 Génération du token

  • Via Postman :
    Configurez une requête de type POST vers l’URL d’authentification avec les identifiants fournis dans la clé de service. Cela vous permettra de récupérer un token temporaire pour tester les appels API manuellement.
  • Depuis SAP S/4HANA :
    La connexion se configure dans la transaction SM59, via une destination de type HTTP (Type G) que nous voyons après en détail.
  1. Affectation des rôles

Attribuez les rôles requis à l’utilisateur (via la Role Collection Market-Rates-User) afin d’accéder à l’interface Fiori du service.

 

Une fois le service actif, l’application SaaS propose trois tuiles principales dans votre Launchpad Fiori :

  • « Configure Default Values »
    Paramètres généraux tels que la durée de rétention des données ou les options d’archivage.
  • « Configure Favorites »
    Sélection des paires de devises ou indices favoris pour chaque utilisateur. Ces favoris seront ensuite affichés dans la vue principale.
  • Display Market Rates
    Visualisation en temps réel des taux de marché récupérés via le service Refinitiv.

 

 

 Lors de la navigation dans l’application, les données sont téléchargées dynamiquement à partir de SAP BTP vers l’interface, selon les instruments sélectionnés.

 

 

Passons maintenant à l’intégration côté système SAP S/4HANA, ici en version On-Premise. Le processus repose sur la configuration d’une acquisition de données via RFC HTTP, combinée à un mapping des instruments financiers.

🔹Remarque : cette intégration s’appuie sur la note SAP suivante :
👉SAP Note 2431370 – Market Data Integration with BTP

 

Étapes de configuration dans SAP GUI

🧭 Customizing : accès

Accédez au chemin suivant dans SPRO :

SAP Financial Supply Chain Management

> Treasury and Risk Management

> Market Data Management

> Data Acquisition

  1. Définir une acquisition de données
  • Créez un identifiant (Data Provider) qui représentera la connexion à Refinitiv.
  • Exemple : « Refinitiv »
  1. Créer une destination RFC (SM59)
  • Type de connexion : G (HTTP vers un serveur externe)
  • URL hôte : celle indiquée dans la clé de service
  • Port : 443
  • Chemin : /oauth/token
  • Authentification :
    • Nom d’utilisateur : Client ID (depuis la clé)
    • Mot de passe : Secret (depuis la clé)
  • SSL : activé avec certificat client standard

⚠️Lors du test de connexion, un code HTTP 400 peut apparaître. Il est attendu et ne bloque pas la suite.

  1. Associer la destination RFC à l’acquisition
  • Programme partenaire : TB_DATAFEED_HCP_ACCESS
  • Mode : Synchrone
  • Destination RFC : celle créée en étape 2
  • URI : downloadURL (dans la clé de service)
  • Scope OAuth : depuis la clé

 

  1. Définir les sources de données

Exemples de sources :

  • ST – données globales Refinitiv fournies par SAP
  1. Configurer les devises et instruments

Pour chaque paire de devises à intégrer :

  • Type d’instruction : 01 – Devises
  • Exemple :
    • EUR~USD:01
    • CHF~JPY:01
  • Propriété du taux : BID, MID, ASK, CLOSE...
  • Taux de variation maximal autorisé (%), sauvegarde permanente, etc.

Une fois l’ensemble des étapes finalisées, votre système SAP est prêt à consommer automatiquement les taux de marché fournis par SAP via Refinitiv. Ces données sont désormais accessibles depuis la transaction TB48 ou exploitées directement dans vos processus métiers tels que le paiement, la conversion de devises ou la valorisation d’actifs.

Dans le cas d’un environnement SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP met également à disposition une tuile Fiori dédiée pour la visualisation et le suivi des taux intégrés

 

 

Le service SAP Market Rates Management simplifie considérablement l’intégration des données financières dans SAP. Grâce à une approche standardisée et sécurisée, il vous permet de vous affranchir des imports manuels ou des intégrations maison souvent lourdes à maintenir.

C’est un excellent point d’entrée pour exploiter les services cloud de SAP BTP dans des contextes hybrides ou full cloud.

entry.title: Customer COE Team Accreditation - From the customer's point of view
entry.content:
In customer projects, we often find that the success of implementing, using, and operating SAP products depends significantly on users having the necessary knowledge of the functionality of the deployed SAP solutions and upcoming product innovations, processes and their efficiency being transparent, and responsibilities being clearly defined. In addition, problems in the project and operation of SAP products are less of a technical nature and are mostly due to non-technical reasons, such as lack of solution acceptance, knowledge gaps, or communication problems.

This is precisely where the Customer COE concept from SAP comes into play: To establish an SAP team of experts across the business and SAP products that brings together the right resources and thus promotes a continuous exchange of information within the company.

In line with the SAP product portfolio, the Customer COE concept has also continued to evolve, but its intention has remained: to combine SAP expertise and competencies in the company and to bring togehter SAP experts across all areas. The resulting knowledge and network enable the Customer COE to continuously manage and drive improvements and innovations centrally – and the SAP product across and deployment independently.

 

Customer COE Team Accreditation from SAP - a summary

We have already reported in the Customer COE Community Blog on the added value of a Customer COE in the customer company and about the team accreditation mentioned in the same breath.

In summary, our team accreditation provides our customers every two years with guidance on which topics are well established as a foundation, which still need to be addressed, and which remain unanswered. Why every two years? Quite simply – nothing is more constant than change. A regular review is recommended, and every two years has proven to be a successful approach. Of course, an audit can also be requested during the year, for example, if business units are merged or other major organizational or technical changes occur. Here, too, preparing for team accreditation helps to sit down with the relevant SAP experts within the company - from IT and business - and coordinate the new structure.

In addition to the orientation of which topics are important, the result of the team accreditation can be used to measure the maturity of the basic criteria for a successful customer COE. In our experience, this is a good starting point for staying in motion in the continuous improvement process.

Another aspect is not to be underestimated, namely that of “internal marketing.” Too easily, the “stay on the ball” goes under and competes with prestigious projects. This is precisely where you should be supported by the certificate that you receive after a successful Customer COE Team accreditation and/or the top 10 customer award. In the following, we will discuss this again.

 

“Top10 Team Accreditation” - the perfect starting point to earn a gold star

As already mentioned, ongoing efforts and day-to-day improvements often go unnoticed, overshadowed by the spotlight on high-profile projects or urgent 'rescue missions' once problems have already escalated.

Let us celebrate and regularly showcase the achievements of your SAP expert team.

As part of the annual top 10 team accreditation, the audit results of a calendar year are evaluated globally. The winners of the “Top 10 Team Accreditation” achieved a very good overall result as part of the audit, some even the highest score – 200 out of 200 points. All Customer COE Top 10 winners in the respective calendar year will be contacted personally and will receive a Top10 certificate by post.

The very good performance of the Customer COE audit is a reason to celebrate. Take advantage of the award, pat yourself and your SAP team of experts on the back, and talk about what you can afford in everyday practice.

Use the Customer COE Top10 certificate and communicate your very good performance in your company. This is precisely the intention of the Top 10 award – in line with the principle "do good and tell people about it”.

Congratulations to all Customer COE Top10 winners of the past years!
You have decided to bring together SAP experts in the company to moderate the continuous change process, thus increasing transparency and efficiency in the implementation and the use of SAP software solutions and services.

In 2024, Customer COE were once again able to impress with their excellent audit results. The Customer COEs of the following companies have been recognized, to name a few:

BARMHERZIGE BRÜDER ÖSTERREICH, Cervecería Polar, C.A., Cotemar S.A. de C.V., Dataport, Evonik, Festo SE & Co. KG, KPMG IT Service GmbH, Nord/LB Norddeutsche Landesbank, RWE AG, Universität Basel, Vattenfall.

 

Experience of successful Customer COEs

Customer COEs – according to the intension of SAP – combine SAP expertise in the company and bring it together across all areas. In this way, the opportunities and challenges of emerging changes can be proactively shaped at an early stage

What have experienced Customer COEs learned, and how have regular team accreditations and other Customer COE services supported their success?

 

KPMG IT Service
“Accreditation offers us an excellent opportunity to work professionally with SAP at expert level, as well as to provide excellent support for our SAP applications and business processes and to develop them innovatively. The SAP award is a very great motivation for all our employees at KPMG Deutschland’s “SAP Center of Excellence”, a recognition of our performance, and proudly fulfils us.

Oliver Kuhne, Strategy & Service Management, KPMG IT Service GmbH
Top10 Winner 2024

Informations-Verarbeitungs-Zentrum (IVZ) /Kooperation von ARD und Deutschlandradio
“On the way to the S/4 world, we also reorganized ourselves and aligned directly with the Customer COE Framework. The framework and accompanying service from SAP Business Transformation (BT&A) helped us establish new collaboration models, strengthen governance, and strengthen processes, among other things. Meanwhile, we have also reached a very high degree of maturity here, which perfectly reflects the excellent result of our last team accreditation. But our journey is not over. The further expansion of cloud integrations and the inclusion of AI possibilities and the associated adaptation of our organization are only two of our future topics. “

Gil Ritter, SAP Applications, Head of Department / Customer COE Manager
Top 10 Winners 2024

Customer COE des Land Hessen (Hessisches Competence Center, HCC)
Customer COE of the German State of Hessen
SAP’s best-practice approach, now known as the Customer COE, has always provided us with important suggestions and decision support in setting up and developing our organization and processes in the HCC. It is a matter of course for us that we take the two-year Customer COE team accreditation, as we benefit greatly from it. On the one hand, the preparation contested by a large employee subgroup is a task that strongly promotes cohesion. With the activities, we also take stock of our activities over the past two years, as the audit measures our maturity level. On the other hand, there is the SAP Customer COE Quick Assessment Service (two-day workshop). Of course, we are on the test and are evaluated. However, we also receive incentives for reflection and sound comments, which in turn give us valuable impulses for our further development. Our serious approach has so far always been reflected in good results. The last six times, we each achieved the maximum number of points, which as a success of all employees also goes beyond the HCC.

Elisabeth Degoutrie, Head of Customer COE Management
Top 10 Winners 2023

Stadtwerke Potsdam GmbH
“We, from Stadtwerke Potsdam, are extremely proud and unbelievably happy to have received the award from SAP as one of the “Best 10 Customer COEs”. This honour is an exceptional recognition for the achievements of the entire team in recent years. Since 2004, we successfully undergo the certification/ team accreditation for SAP Customer COE every two years. The entire program provides us with valuable impulses regarding SAP’s strategy, new architecture concepts, and the modern operation of an SAP landscape. With these insights, we continuously evolve our organization and operations. Meanwhile, we have reached a highly professional level, for which we received this great award. It motivates all of us to continue along the path we have taken. “

Torsten Püplichhuisen, Head of Application Department, Stadwerke Potsdam
Top10 Winner 2023

Deutsche Rentenversicherung  (DRV)
“The SAP Customer COE program has always encouraged us to take new paths. No easy undertaking in the public sector. We are on the road with our Customer COE on these new paths – with agile approach, where it fits, with lived BizDevOps to realize our business requirements faster and with first steps to the cloud. The SAP Customer COE Team Accreditation (formerly certification), which lasts every two years, was awarded 200 points this time. It helps us – especially our customers – to position us as a competent team in the SAP environment.  Public service – and it’s all about it! “

Marko Stamatovic, IT domain personnel, German pension insurance
Top10 Winner 2023

regio iT gesellschaft für informationstechnologie mbh
“For us as a service provider, excellent service is very important. Our motto: “Delight customers! “. The renewed recognition of our SAP Customer COE in the top 10 is also important for our customers and a guarantee that you have chosen the right partner for all SAP matters and topics. For us! “

Daniel Geers, Center Manager SAP Platform & Operations, regio iT gesellschaft für informationtechnologie mbh
Top 10 Winners 2023

 

You’re curious now and want to start your own Customer COE journey?

Reach out to us anytime. In our Customer COE Portal Media Library, you can find support offerings for the setup and further development of your Customer COE under the chapter “Service”. Your SAP contacts and the SAP Customer COE program team  will be happy to help you find the right support for you.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025: A Celebration of Innovation, Community, and the Future of SAP
entry.content:
Salvador, with its rich history as the first capital of Brazil, is a city renowned for its vibrant culture, stunning colonial architecture, and profound roots in shaping Brazil’s national identity. Just as Salvador’s history of blending influences to create something unique, this SAP Inside Track event brought together diverse minds to discuss the future of enterprise technology, share success stories, and inspire one another.

The event was packed with insightful lectures and an enthusiastic audience, showing that the SAP community in Brazil is stronger than ever. Let’s take a look at the highlights:

Marssel Vilaça: The Power of the SAP Community

Marssel opened the day by reminding everyone of the incredible strength that lies within the SAP Community. He emphasized how collaboration, knowledge sharing, and active community participation can elevate careers and projects. His inspiring words set the perfect tone for the day.

Fabio Dezan: The New Brazil Tax Legislation and SAP Challenges

Fabio tackled one of the most pressing topics: the recent updates to Brazil’s complex tax legislation. He shared practical challenges and insights on how SAP systems, especially S/4HANA, must adapt to comply with these new rules. It was a highly technical yet accessible session that added significant value for consultants and customers alike.

Aline Damasceno: Stocks Management Using S/4HANA

Aline delivered an excellent lecture focused on optimizing stock and inventory management processes within S/4HANA. She demonstrated how modern functionalities can bring visibility, agility, and efficiency to supply chain operations —a crucial topic for industries facing volatile demand and supply conditions.

Manuel Robalinho(me): Finance APIs and Gen AI for Cash Flow

Manuel’s session was one of the most anticipated. He explained how Finance APIs can streamline integrations for Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, and Bank Statement processing with Brazilian banks. He also brought a futuristic angle by presenting how Generative AI can predict and optimize cash flow operations — a real glimpse into the future of finance!

Illiada Campos: PM Module Success Story

Illiada captivated everyone with a fantastic case study combining standard SAP functionalities with the Plant Maintenance (PM) Module. Her presentation showed how a strategic use of what’s already available in SAP can achieve massive project success without excessive customization — a key Clean Core principle.

Elianna Lanna: S/4HANA Greenfield at PetroRecôncavo

Elianna gave a powerful presentation about the Greenfield implementation of S/4HANA at PetroRecôncavo. She walked the audience through the project's major challenges, including change management, data migration, and aligning business processes with the new system, showing the resilience and adaptability needed for large-scale transformations.

Luciano Casado: Joule and S/4HANA Public Cloud

Luciano impressed the audience by showing the integration of Joule, SAP’s Business AI, with S/4HANA Public Cloud. His lecture demonstrated how AI is not just a trend, but a tool to significantly improve decision-making and automate routine tasks, providing a real competitive advantage.

Lucas Ferreira: Fiori and Clean Core Using Tachyonix

Lucas gave a masterclass on the importance of the Clean Core strategy and how Fiori apps can be customized efficiently using the Tachyonix product. He emphasized the importance of minimizing modifications to maintain the system's scalability and upgradability, a message that resonated strongly with the audience.

Otto: SAP Accelerate by Accenture

Otto closed the lectures with a dynamic talk about SAP Accelerate, a product developed by Accenture to speed up SAP project deliveries while maintaining quality. His lecture showed real cases where time-to-value was significantly improved, impressing everyone with practical results.

An Unforgettable Audience

The event was marked by a fantastic turnout — a true testament to the passion of the SAP community in Salvador and Brazil. Participants were highly engaged, asking questions, sharing experiences, and demonstrating that collaboration and knowledge sharing are alive and well.

There was an increase in female participation in the lectures at this SIT. Fifty percent of the lectures were presented by SAP consultants who shared their success stories. Amazing!

See You in 2026!

Given the success of this year’s event, the organizers announced that SAP Inside Track Salvador will return in 2026, even bigger and better. The goal is to continue building this community, expanding its topics, and introducing even more innovation and collaboration to the SAP ecosystem in Brazil.

Congratulations to everyone involved — speakers, participants, organizers, and sponsors — for making SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025 an unforgettable experience!

#SAPCommunity #SAPInsideTrack #SITSAL #SAPS4HANA #SAPFiori #CleanCore #FinanceAPIs #GenAI #SAPPublicCloud #SAPAccelerate  #BrazilTaxUpdate

entry.title: Discover the new SAP University Alliances Global Board at SAP Community!
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce a valuable new resource to our academic community: the SAP University Alliances Global Community Board. A space to connect, learn, and share – globally.

This global platform is designed for professors, researchers, students, and education enthusiasts to explore content related to SAP technologies, teaching innovation, and academic collaboration at an international level.

Just like our SAP Community Board for Latin America, Portugal, and Spain, this space follows the same familiar structure – featuring Discussions, Blogposts, and Academic Content & Resources – now with a broader, global scope.

What can you find there?

  • Discussions – A great space to connect with colleagues from other countries, exchange experiences, and start conversations around shared challenges and opportunities.
  • Blogposts – Explore inspiring articles from SAP, educators and students.
  • Academic Content & Resources – Access valuable materials for classroom and research use.
  • Featured Topics – Stay updated with curated posts on trends, tools, and opportunities.

This global board is the perfect complement to our regional space. While the Latin America, Portugal, and Spain board focuses on localized collaboration, the global version brings together voices from across the world – helping spark new partnerships and insights across borders.

📌If you're already part of the SAP University Alliances Community, just save the page to your bookmarks for quick and easy access whenever you need it.

👉Start exploring: SAP University Alliances Global Board
